title: plan-executor compiled-plan manifest
description: Structured output of the plan-executor:compile-plan skill. Read and dispatched deterministically by the Rust scheduler. One LLM call produces this; no LLM invocation should mutate it after compile time.

version.description: Schema version. Bump on breaking changes.

type.description: Plan type drives default review/validation sets.

jira.description: JIRA ticket ID (e.g. CCP-123). Empty string if none.

target_repo.description: GitHub owner/repo slug where changes will be committed. Null for local-only runs.

target_branch.description: Feature branch to create/checkout for this plan.

waves.description: Ordered list of execution waves. Each wave runs its task_ids in parallel. A wave starts when every wave in its depends_on array has completed successfully.

id.description: Unique wave ID. Fix-loop waves use IDs >= 100.

tasks.description: Map from task_id -> task spec. Every task_id referenced in waves[].task_ids MUST exist here.

prompt_file.description: Relative path (from tasks.json's directory) to the sub-task prompt markdown.

agent_type.description: Dispatcher picks the CLI based on this value. Enum is locked to what handoff.rs::AgentType supports.

can_fail.description: If true, a non-zero exit from this sub-agent is logged but does not fail the wave. Used for reviewer-diversity tasks (codex/gemini) where the tool may be unavailable.

description.description: One-line human summary. Not read by the executor; for the compile-then-inspect step.
